sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the maze clearly:
- The gray room has corridors separated by charcoal walls (#)
- There's a vertical wall at cols 24-28 (relative: 10-14), rows 30-39
- There's a horizontal wall at rows 40-44, cols 14-23 (relative: 0-9)
- The block (OOOOO/BBBBB) is at rows 40-44, cols 34-38
- The white cross (W/w) is at rows 31-33, cols 20-22
The maze structure:
```
Rows 25-29: open corridor (full width)
Rows 30-39: split by vertical wall - left chamber (with white cross) and right corridor
Rows 40-44: left side has horizontal wall, right side has the block
Rows 45-49: open corridor (full width)
```
The block is 5x5. It can move in corridors. Let me think about where it can go:
- Currently at rows 40-44, cols 34-38 (right side)
- Can move RIGHT to go further right
- Can move LEFT but the wall at cols 24-28 blocks it
- Can move UP to go to rows 35-39
- Can move DOWN to go to rows 45-49
Wait, I need to reconsider. When I moved UP, the block went from rows 45-49 to rows 40-44. But rows 40-44 have the horizontal wall on the left. The block is on the right side so it can still move there.
Let me think about the goal. The top box (rows 9-15, cols 33-39) has a blue shape. The bottom-left box (rows 53-62, cols 1-10) has a blue "F" shape. These might be reference patterns.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, I think the white cross might be the actual player, and the orange/blue block might be something else. But the arrow keys moved the orange/blue block, not the white cross...
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the game is about moving the block to a specific position where it "fits" or matches a pattern. The top box shows a blue shape that could be the target configuration.
Let me try to navigate the block to different positions and see what happens. Let me first try moving it UP again to see if it goes further.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model updated: The maze has a vertical wall at cols 24-28, rows 30-39 and a horizontal wall at rows 40-44, cols 14-23. The 5x5 block is at rows 40-44, cols 34-38. The white cross is at rows 31-33, cols 20-22. The block slides when arrow keys are pressed.
Plan: Try moving the block UP again to explore the upper corridor, then figure out the goal.

</tool_call>
